compiled_state.title: Add brainstorm:complete tag for sprint epic selection
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Practitioners running `/dso:sprint` see an epic list gated by `--min-children=1` — a proxy for "decomposed and ready to execute." The proxy lets decomposed-but-unbrainstormed epics appear (a scrutiny gap) and hides brainstormed epics that haven't yet been decomposed (which sprint's Preplanning Gate would handle automatically). There is no durable ticket-level evidence of brainstorm completion — only a session-scoped sentinel file.

## Success Criteria
- /dso:brainstorm writes the `brainstorm:complete` tag on the epic at Phase 3 completion and removes any `scrutiny:pending` tag in the same `ticket edit --tags=` operation (read-merge-write pattern preserves other tags).
- sprint-list-epics.sh `--has-tag=<tag>` (net-new flag) returns only tickets carrying that tag; `_build_index()` includes each ticket's `tags` field. /dso:sprint passes `--has-tag=brainstorm:complete` in place of `--min-children=1`, and epics with the tag but zero children appear in the selectable list.
- When /dso:sprint (no args) produces its hidden-epics note, the message contains the string `/dso:brainstorm` and directs the practitioner to run it on untagged epics (replacing the current "0 children" wording).
- A one-shot migration phase inside plugins/dso/scripts/update-artifacts.sh (invoked by the existing `dso update-artifacts` shim) iterates every open epic via the ticket reducer, greps each for the `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading in description AND comment event bodies, writes the tag for matches using read-merge-write, and prints one `UNMATCHED: <epic-id>` line to stdout per epic without a PIL match; exit code is 0 on successful completion regardless of unmatched count.
- Running the migration twice against the same .tickets-tracker/ state produces identical tag state and identical `UNMATCHED:` stdout output. Marker `.claude/.brainstorm-tag-migration-v1` makes the second run a no-op exit 0. Migration is tag-only and self-guards against execution in the plugin source repo (no-op exit with a logged notice).
- When /dso:brainstorm is invoked with no arguments, its epic-selection list surfaces open epics that have children but lack the `brainstorm:complete` tag (the scrutiny-gap set) as high-value brainstorm candidates alongside the existing zero-child list.
- Against a fixture mixing PIL-bearing and PIL-absent open epics, the end-to-end flow verifies: (a) migration tags exactly the PIL-bearing set and reports the PIL-absent set via `UNMATCHED:` lines; (b) sprint-list-epics.sh `--has-tag=brainstorm:complete` output matches the tagged set; (c) /dso:sprint (no args) displays only the tagged set and its hidden-epics note contains `/dso:brainstorm`.

## Dependencies
None (self-contained). Implicit assumptions: the ticket reducer can access comment event bodies (current capability), and the ticket CLI's `ticket show` output exposes the `tags` field (current capability).

## Approach
Introduce `brainstorm:complete` as a positive ticket tag. Brainstorm Phase 3 writes it via read-merge-write (preserving other tags, removing `scrutiny:pending`). Extend sprint-list-epics.sh with `--has-tag=<tag>` and include `tags` in the index builder. Swap sprint's epic-selection filter. Add a versioned idempotent migration phase to update-artifacts.sh using the ticket reducer + PIL-heading grep across description and comment event bodies. Brainstorm's no-arg list highlights children-without-tag epics as scrutiny-gap candidates.

## Scenario Analysis
- Atomic tag edit drops existing tags (high, runtime): ticket edit --tags= is full replacement; must read-merge-write. Addressed by SC1.
- --has-tag filter receives no tag data from index builder (critical, runtime): _build_index() must include tags. Addressed by SC2.
- Migration grep misses PIL in comment events (high, runtime): comments stored as JSON event files; grep must target description AND comment event bodies. Addressed by SC4.
- Marker-after-all-edits leaves partial state on error (high, deployment): re-run must verify per-epic tag state via reducer. Addressed by SC5.
- Migration executes in plugin source repo (high, deployment): must self-guard. Addressed by SC5.
- Decomposed-without-brainstorm epics permanently invisible to sprint (high, configuration): scrutiny-gap recovery path. Addressed by SC6.

comments.body:
### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: migration/compatibility (gracefully degraded — external research offered no actionable insight for well-established idempotent tag-write pattern; existing update-artifacts.sh idempotence pattern confirmed sufficient)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 6
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (brainstorm SKILL.md, sprint SKILL.md). Step 5 execution skipped (not HARD-GATE required).
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: not applicable (no volume decision)
comments.author: Claude
